title: EB1 Visa Requirements
content:
There are 40,000 visas annually available under this category. Priority workers include:
 1. aliens of extraordinary ability in the arts, sciences, business, education and athletics (EB1-A);
 2. outstanding professors and researchers (EB1-B); and
 3. managers and executives of international companies being transferred permanently to the U.S. (EB1-C).

title: EB1 Visa Requirements
content: For aliens of extraordinary ability (EB1-A), not only labor certification is not required but the foreign person does not need to be sponsored by a U.S. employer. These foreign nationals may petition themselves. However, beneficiaries of EB1-B and EB1-C need sponsorship from a U.S. employer.

title: EB1 Visa Requirements
content:
| | EB1-A: 
Aliens of Extraordinary Ability | EB1-B: 
Outstanding Professors and Researchers | EB1-C: 
International Managers and Executives | 
| --- | --- | --- | --- | 
| Labor Certification | Not required | Not required | Not required | 
| Self petition | Allowed | Not Allowed | Not Allowed | 
| Qualifications | Extraordinary ability in sciences, arts, education, business, or athletics, coming to the States to continue the work in the same field, and work will benefit the U.S. | Having obtained international acclaim; Having three year experience in teaching and research; and being on a tenured track or comparable position | Having been employed in a managerial or executive capacity for at least one continuous year in the preceding three years by the overseas affiliate of the U.S. employer; and coming to work in the United States in a managerial or executive capacity |

title: Success Story: From Biomedical Disease Research to I-140 EB-1B Approval for a Biomedical Scientist
content:
**Clientâ€™s Testimonial:**
_"Thank you so much for all the support in getting the I-140 application approved.â€_
* * *
**On March 16 th, 2026, we received another EB-1B (Outstanding Professors and Researchers) approval for a Senior Scientist in the Field of Biomedical Science (Approval Notice).**
* * *
**General Field:** Biomedical Science
**Position at the Time of Case Filing:** Senior Scientist
**Country of Origin:** India
**State of Residence at the Time of Filing:** California
**Approval Notice Date: **March 16th, 2026
**Processing Time:** 13 months, 23 days
* * *
**Case Summary:**
A biomedical scientist received I-140 EB-1B approval for her work in biomedical science, with a research record focused on immunology, disease pathogenesis, and metabolism. The client, who holds a Ph.D., currently works as a senior scientist developing human genetic therapeutics for rare diseases. North America Immigration Law Group (Chen Immigration Law Associates) presented the case by showing that her work addressed a practical and medically important need, improving scientific understanding of serious diseases such as tuberculosis, HIV-related complications, diabetes, asthma, liver fibrosis, and rare muscular disorders. The petition emphasized that the clientâ€™s work had significance beyond a narrow research specialty. Her studies helped clarify disease mechanisms, immune responses, and therapeutic development pathways, including analytical assay development supporting clinical-stage research for a rare genetic disease. As one recommendation letter explained: â€œ _[Clientâ€™s] research is thus clearly in the best interest of the nation_.â€ This framing helped show that her work had both scientific importance and practical relevance for future treatment development. A major strength of the case was the clientâ€™s consistent record of advancing biomedical research across multiple disease areas. Her published work on immune responses to tuberculosis, metabolic regulation, asthma-related airway mechanisms, and HIV-related liver fibrosis showed a sustained pattern of identifying biological mechanisms that other researchers could apply, test, and extend. The petition also connected her work to broader U.S. public health priorities, noting evidence of support from major funding sources including _the American Heart Association, the National Institutes of Health, and the National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute._
To show that the client was internationally recognized as outstanding in the field, we documented 8 peer-reviewed scientific articles, 1 Worldwide Protein Data Bank entry, 237 citations, and at least 30 completed peer reviews.

title: Success Story: From Biomedical Disease Research to I-140 EB-1B Approval for a Biomedical Scientist
content:
Rather than treating these metrics as automatically sufficient, we explained that the citation record reflected independent reliance by researchers in the United States and abroad, while the peer-review record showed that journals trusted the client to evaluate the quality and validity of othersâ€™ work. The petition was also supported by 5 recommendation letters, including letters from independent experts familiar with the clientâ€™s work through her publications and field contributions. Together, these letters reinforced the objective evidence and helped demonstrate how her findings had influenced biomedical research and therapeutic development. This approval reflects the strength of a carefully prepared I-140 EB-1B petition built around original scientific contributions, independent reliance, peer trust, and practical biomedical relevance. We were proud to help secure this result for a biomedical scientist whose work supports disease research and the continued advancement of therapeutic innovation in the United States. --------------------------------------------
North America Immigration Law Group (Chen Immigration Law Associates) is a U.S. immigration law firm dedicated to representing corporations, research institutions, and individuals from all 50 U.S. states regarding I-140 immigration petitions. We specialize in employment-based immigration petition and have a proven record of high success rate for the categories of: EB2-NIW (National Interest Waiver), EB1-A (Alien of Extraordinary Ability), EB1-B (Outstanding Researcher/Professor) and O-1 (Alien of Extraordinary Ability). **Our Sixty-four Thousand I-140 Approvals Provide Unprecedented Insight into the USCIS Adjudication Trend**
With nearly 64,000 EB-1A, EB-1B, EB-2 NIW and O-1 cases approved, we have first hand information on the manner in which the USCIS adjudicate I-140 cases. We carefully analyze the data for all of our cases and apply the results of our analyses toward giving our clients up-to-date advice and adapting our strategies such that we remain on par with the ever-shifting landscape of immigration law in the U.S. With us, you will always have access to important updates, strategies, and information so that you can make the most informed decisions about your case. **We Have Helped Tens of Thousands of Clients with Credentials and Backgrounds Similar to Yours**
With our exceedingly large number of successful petitions, no matter what credentials you have, no matter your background and field of expertise, no matter your visa status or nationality, chances are we have helped hundreds or even thousands of clients just like you. Our clients are usually impressed with how well we understand their research and work.

title: Success Story: An Indian Senior Controls Engineer Achieves EB-1A Approval in Just 16 Days
content:
**Clientâ€™s Testimonial:**
_"Thanks for getting this through, appreciate all the efforts and quick turnarounds.â€_
* * *
**On March 18 th, 2026, we received another EB-1A (Alien of Extraordinary Ability) approval for a Senior Motor Control Engineer in the Field of Control of Electrical Drives (Approval Notice).**
* * *
**General Field:** Control of Electrical Drives
**Position at the Time of Case Filing:** Senior Motor Control Engineer
**Country of Origin:** India
**State of Residence at the Time of Filing:** California
**Approval Notice Date: **March 18th, 2026
**Processing Time:** 16 days (Premium Processing Requested)
* * *
**Case Summary:**
We recently represented a Senior Motor Control Engineer from India working in the field of control of electrical drives. His work centers on intelligent motor drive technologies, with particular emphasis on sensorless rotor position estimation, torque optimization, and self-commissioning strategies for synchronous machines. Although highly technical, these areas have clear practical value. Advances in motor control help make electrified transportation, industrial automation, robotics, and renewable-energy systems safer, more efficient, and more reliable. What made this case especially strong was the combination of originality and real-world relevance. Our clientâ€™s work did not stay confined to theory. His research addressed the performance, stability, and efficiency of motor-drive systems in ways that other experts in the field were already using and building upon. **A Record of Achievement That Stood Out**
The evidence showed a sustained pattern of accomplishment. Our client had authored 11 first-authored journal articles and 13 peer-reviewed conference articles, 12 of them first-authored. His work had also drawn broad international attention, with citations from researchers in at least 43 countries. He ranked among the top 1% most highly cited authors publishing on motor control topics over the past 10 years, and his h-index of 13 placed him among the top 3% of authors in electronic engineering. **Contributions That Helped Define the Field**
North America Immigration Law Group (Chen Immigration Law Associates) highlighted several areas where our clientâ€™s work had made a meaningful mark. One major contribution involved improving the efficiency and reliability of complex control systems. By developing novel estimation techniques that reduced the need for physical hardware dependencies, this work supported more cost-effective and robust system designs. Another important area involved enhancing dynamic control and operational stability in advanced machinery.

title: Success Story: An Indian Senior Controls Engineer Achieves EB-1A Approval in Just 16 Days
content:
The client developed methods that allowed for highly accurate performance metrics even under fluctuating operating conditionsâ€”advances that are highly valuable in settings requiring precise control. The client also advanced automated calibration and monitoring methods, achieving exceptional accuracy. These improvements supported more efficient system deployment, stronger fault detection, and better long-term operational performance. **Recognition from the Field**
This case was also strengthened by substantial evidence of peer recognition. Our client had completed at least 91 peer reviews for major journals in the field and had been selected as a star reviewer. These are meaningful signals of trust, showing that other experts and leading journals recognized his authority and expertise. **The Approval**
This approval reflected the strength of a record built on original contributions, sustained recognition, and practical influence in a highly important engineering field. We are delighted that we could help present this extraordinary case successfully. --------------------------------------------

title: Success Story: From Ph.D. Student to Permanent Residency! EB-1A Approved for a Chinese Robotics Expert without RFE
content:
**Clientâ€™s Testimonial:**
_"I am incredibly grateful to the entire team atNorth America Immigration Law Group for their unwavering support and dedication throughout my EB-1A journey. From start to finish, they were always available to answer my questions, guide me through every step of the process, and ensure that my case was presented in the strongest possible way.â€_
* * *
**On March 19 th, 2026, we received an EB-1A (Alien of Extraordinary Ability) approval for a Ph.D. Student in the Field of Robotics (Approval Notice).**
* * *
**General Field:** Robotics
**Position at the Time of Case Filing:** Ph.D. Student
**Country of Origin:** China
**State of Residence at the Time of Filing:** Georgia
**Approval Notice Date: **March 19th, 2026
**Processing Time:** 16 months, 22 days
* * *
**Case Summary:**
North America Immigration Law Group (Chen Immigration Law Associates) successfully secured EB-1A approval for a Chinese Ph.D. student specializing in robotics, holding an M.S. in electrical and computer engineering, with the petition approved in over 16 months. This case centered on presenting a highly technical robotics profile, one focused on AI-powered robotic manipulation, computer vision, and deep learning, in a way that clearly demonstrated both field-wide influence and sustained national and international acclaim. The researcher's work spans robotic grasping detection, affordance recognition, and multimodal assistive robot systems. The strategic objective was to translate the client's early-career research record into a coherent narrative of extraordinary ability: one where citation impact, publication quality, peer review service, and independent expert validation all pointed to the same conclusion. **Scientific Leadership and External Validation**
The expert's research has received competitive funding from the _National Science Foundation (NSF)_ , affirming that the work has been evaluated favorably through rigorous external review and recognized as aligned with U.S. national scientific priorities. The NSF's investment in the client's research on assistive robotic systems reflects the broader importance of this field to U.S. interests in healthcare, manufacturing, and autonomous technology leadership. Four expert recommendation letters were submitted in support of the petition. One expert has noted that:
_â€œAdditionally, [Client]â€™s research aligns with the United Statesâ€™ ongoing efforts to advance robotics technology and maintain leadership in the global robotics industry.

title: Success Story: Securing EB-1A Approval for an Earth Science Expert: Overcoming an RFE with Strategic Advocacy
content:
**Clientâ€™s Testimonial:**
_"Thanks again for your support throughout this process.â€_
* * *
**On March 3 rd, 2026, we received another EB-1A (Alien of Extraordinary Ability) approval for a Postdoctoral Fellow in the Field of Earth Science - Hydrology (Approval Notice).**
* * *
**General Field:** Earth Science - Hydrology
**Position at the Time of Case Filing:** Postdoctoral Fellow
**Country of Origin:** China
**State of Residence at the Time of Filing:** Idaho
**Approval Notice Date: **March 3rd, 2026
**Processing Time:** 3 months, 21 days (Premium Processing Upgrade Requested)
* * *
**Case Summary:**
North America Immigration Law Group (Chen Immigration Law Associates) is excited to share the recent EB-1A (Alien of Extraordinary Ability) approval for a Postdoctoral Fellow. Originally from China, our client holds a Ph.D. in civil and environmental engineering and focuses his research on the highly critical field of Earth science - hydrology. We successfully navigated a Request for Evidence (RFE) to secure his approval on March 3rd, 2026, culminating in a total processing time of just 3 months and 21 days. **A Strategic Rebuttal to Secure U.S. Interests**
Following the initial petition filing on November 10, 2025, USCIS issued an RFE on November 20, 2025, seeking additional clarification on how the petitionerâ€™s entry would substantially benefit the United States prospectively. We detailed how his advanced soil moisture retrieval algorithms directly enhance U.S. disaster resilience, wildfire risk prediction, and agricultural productivity. Emphasizing this immense practical value, an independent expert noted:
_"By integrating molecular-scale chemistry with watershed-scale hydrology, [Client]â€™s work provides actionable tools for water managers, agricultural systems, and public health authorities to safeguard water supplies, improve ecological resilience, and build national capacity to manage drought, pollution, and long-term water security."_
**Extraordinary Scientific Contributions and Global Reach**
To unequivocally satisfy the stringent EB-1A criteria, we presented a meticulously documented portfolio of the clientâ€™s scholarly achievements. His prolific research output includes 18 peer-reviewed journal articles, 1 first-authored peer-reviewed conference article, 12 abstracts, and 1 first-authored preprint. Due to its tremendous applicability in the field of Earth science - hydrology, his work has been cited 755 times by independent scholars globally. Furthermore, his authoritative standing is evidenced by his extensive peer review service; he has evaluated the work of his peers in at least 29 reviews for journals and serves on the editorial board as a review editor.

title: Success Story: Securing EB-1A Approval for an Earth Science Expert: Overcoming an RFE with Strategic Advocacy
content:
**Backing from the World's Premier Institutions**
Further solidifying his status at the very top of his field, we highlighted the exceptional financial support his research has garnered. His pioneering projects have been funded by a remarkable array of prestigious organizations, including _the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), the U.S. Forest Service, the U.S. Department of Agriculture, the Australian Research Council, the National Science Foundation of China_ , and _the National Key Research and Development Program of China._
By effectively contextualizing his specialized expertise, publication record, and vital international funding, we successfully demonstrated that the petitioner is an invaluable asset to the United States. --------------------------------------------

title: Success Story: Rapid EB-1A Approval for an Organic Chemistry Researcher Advancing Drug Development
content:
**Clientâ€™s Testimonial:**
_"Thanks for getting this through, appreciate all the efforts and quick turnarounds.â€_
* * *
**On March 28 th, 2026, we received another EB-1A (Alien of Extraordinary Ability) approval for a Senior Research and Development Chemist in the Field of Organic Chemistry (Approval Notice).**
* * *
**General Field:** Organic Chemistry
**Position at the Time of Case Filing:** Senior Research and Development Chemist
**Country of Origin:** India
**State of Residence at the Time of Filing:** Tennessee
**Approval Notice Date:** March 28th, 2026
**Processing Time:** 1 month, 16 days
* * *
**Case Summary:**
The client has established himself as a leading researcher in organic chemistry, with particular expertise in chemical compound analysis, organic synthesis, and stereochemistry. His work has directly contributed to improving the efficiency and accessibility of pharmaceutical compound development, especially through innovations in catalysis and reaction design. His research has been supported by prestigious funding sources, including _the National Institutes of Health_ and _the American Chemical Society (ACS) Petroleum Research Fund_ , reflecting institutional confidence in the importance and applicability of his work. His scholarly output includes **13 peer-reviewed journal articles, along with 1 conference abstract, 1 book chapter, and 2 patents**. His work has been cited **354 times** by researchers across at least 40 countries, confirming its global influence. Notably, one of his publications ranked among the top 10% most-cited articles in Chemistry for its year of publication. This level of citation performance reflects sustained reliance by other experts and underscores the significance of his original contributions. In addition to his publication record, the client has contributed to the field through peer review service. He has completed at least **8 peer reviews** for respected journals. This role as a reviewer demonstrates that leading journals trust his expertise to evaluate the work of other researchers, further confirming his standing in the field. **Original Contributions of Major Significance**
The clientâ€™s research contributions have addressed key challenges in chemical synthesis and drug development. His work established the viability of highly cost-effective catalysts for synthesizing complex molecular structures, reducing reliance on expensive materials and improving accessibility in pharmaceutical and industrial applications. He also developed advanced catalytic methods to enhance the efficiency and selectivity of synthesizing pharmaceutically active compounds, contributing to more sustainable and precise drug development processes. In another major contribution, he advanced precision synthesis techniques for essential molecular building blocks.

title: Success Story: Rapid EB-1A Approval for an Organic Chemistry Researcher Advancing Drug Development
content:
Because these compounds are foundational for diverse antiviral, antibacterial, and anticancer therapies, his improvements to their synthesis directly support the development of more effective and accessible therapeutic solutions. **Letters of Recommendation**
The recommendation letters in this case provided strong support for the objective record and reinforced the clientâ€™s standing in the field. Experts described his work as original, highly influential, and important to continued progress in organic and medicinal chemistry. As one recommender noted, _â€œHis findings are crucial to providing novel drug developments, and the nation would be remiss if it did not allow for his research to continue.â€_
These letters were especially persuasive because they aligned closely with the objective evidence, including the clientâ€™s publication history, citation record, peer review activity, patents, and funded research. **EB-1A Approval and Outlook**
This EB-1A approval reflects a well-documented record of originality, independent recognition, and sustained influence in organic chemistry. This outcome represents an important milestone in the clientâ€™s immigration journey. North America Immigration Law Group (Chen Immigration Law Associates) is proud to have assisted in presenting a case that clearly demonstrated extraordinary ability under the EB-1A standard. --------------------------------------------
